entry.title: Consuming API in RAP for Business Partner
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am using the Onpremise system where i have built RAP on top of this API for business partner.

In gateway test client service works fine and no issue in it.

I have built the class with required interface of RAP and when i run the class system goes 404 error.

Kindly suggest some idea on this. Thanks.

Issue is as below:

1. In the last statment of this code, when i debug the standard on the list of Entity set...I am not finding ENTITY as BUSINESS_PARTNER .

Am i missing anything on the below code ?

Service is already in active status.

I am not getting the response and it end up with error 404 .

cl_http_client=>create_by_destination( EXPORTING destination = 'NONE'

IMPORTING client = DATA(lo_http_client)

EXCEPTIONS argument_not_found = 1

destination_not_found = 2

destination_no_authority = 3

plugin_not_active = 4

OTHERS = 5 ).

 

 

lo_client_proxy = /iwbep/cl_cp_client_proxy_fact=>create_v2_remote_proxy(

EXPORTING

is_proxy_model_key = VALUE #( repository_id = 'DEFAULT'

proxy_model_id = 'ZABC'

proxy_model_version = '0001' )

io_http_client = lo_http_client

iv_relative_service_root = '/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/' ).

 

" Navigate to the resource

lo_request = lo_client_proxy->create_resource_for_entity_set( 'BUSINESS_PARTNER' )->create_request_for_read( ).

Regards,

Karthik S

entry.title: Reprocess OData bulk records
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I'm trying to post 1000 records into s/4 system using odata v2 adapter (batch mode) and suppose I see connection issue after processing 500 records then from 501th record and beyond them all the records gets stopped and goes into exception and flow falls into failed status. again we have to reprocess and duplicates will be into s/4. How do we reprocess from 501th records automatically if such connection issue occurs?

I'm facing timeout issue as 1000 records are not been processed in 10min. I had to rerun and duplicates are sent but overwritten luckily still wanted to check if that's not happening in s/4!

entry.title: SAP Settlement Management – Retrieve PDF Form via API for Lobster Integration (XInvoice/ZUGFeRD)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I'm facing an integration challenge in the area of SAP Settlement Management (Condition Contract Management) and hope someone has had similar experiences or can point me in the right direction.

Background:

My customer uses Lobster as their EDI/middleware solution. For standard billing, the integration is already working: Lobster retrieves the invoice PDF via the standard OData API:

 
 
GET /sap/opu/odata/SAP/API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV/GetPDF

This works smoothly and is well documented.

The Problem:

Now we need to build an analogous process for the Customer Rebate Document from Settlement Management – i.e., the document issued to the customer during bonus settlement (Customer Rebate Settlement Document / credit memo equivalent).

The goal is to also retrieve this form via an interface and pass it to Lobster, so it can be further processed and sent as an XInvoice (XRechnung) or ZUGFeRD-compliant electronic invoice.

My Research So Far:

I have searched extensively but have not found a comparable standard API:

  • On the SAP API Business Hub (api.sap.com), there does not appear to be a dedicated OData API for Settlement Management forms / Condition Contract documents
  • API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV only covers classic SD billing documents, not Settlement Management documents
  • I am not aware of any RFC-enabled function modules that cover this use case

My Questions for the Community:

  1. Is there a (perhaps lesser-known) OData or REST API in the SAP standard that allows programmatic retrieval of a Settlement Management form PDF?
  2. Would an RFC/BAPI approach be feasible?
  3. Are there alternative integration approaches, such as:
    • Direct integration via SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) / Integration Suite?
    • Output via SPAD/Spool with subsequent retrieval via web service?
    • Using Smart Forms / Adobe Forms Output Management with a downstream retrieval step?
  4. Has anyone successfully implemented this process – rebate settlement document → middleware → XInvoice/ZUGFeRD delivery – and can share their approach?

System Environment: SAP S/4HANA (On-Premise), Lobster as middleware, target format XRechnung / ZUGFeRD 2.x

Any hint is appreciated – even a confirmed "this is not possible in standard" would already be helpful and move us forward. Thanks in advance!

Best regards

entry.title: CAP Node.js: Add authorization filter without breaking OData options
entry.content:
Hi all,

I’m working with SAP CAP (Node.js) exposing OData services consumed by a SAPUI5 SmartTable.

I have a data model with a many-to-many relationship:

  • A MainEntity can have N SubEntities
  • A SubEntity can belong to M MainEntities

Example (simplified):

entity MainEntity {
  key ID : UUID;
  name   : String;
  subs   : Composition of many MainSub on subs.main = $self;
}

entity MainSub {
  key ID   : UUID;
  main     : Association to MainEntity;
  subCode  : String;
}

 Requirement

I need to implement row-level authorization in the backend such that:

A user can only see MainEntities that are linked to SubEntities assigned to that user.

Conceptually:

WHERE EXISTS (
  SELECT 1
  FROM MainSub s
  WHERE s.main_ID = MainEntity.ID
    AND s.subCode IN (userAllowedSubs)
)

Problem:

The frontend (SmartTable) sends dynamic OData queries like:

$filter=contains(name,'ABC')
&$orderby=name asc
&$top=100
&$skip=0

If I override the query in a before READ handler like:

req.query.SELECT.where = ...

 I break the original OData query, losing filtering, sorting, paging, etc.

I’m looking for the correct CAP pattern to:

  • Inject an authorization restriction (AND condition)
  • Preserve all incoming OData query options:
    • $filter
    • $orderby
    • $top / $skip
    • $select
  • Work correctly with SAPUI5 SmartTable

Expected behavior

Final query should behave like:

WHERE
  <original OData filter>
  AND EXISTS (authorization condition)

Additional context

This is conceptually similar to row-level authorization in SAP ERP (e.g. restricting data visibility based on organizational assignments), but implemented in CAP.

Questions

  1. What is the recommended way in CAP to append conditions to the existing CQN instead of replacing it?
  2. Is using req.query.where(...) with EXISTS the correct approach?
  3. Are there better patterns (e.g. CDS views, aspects, annotations) for this type of authorization?

Any guidance or best practices would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!!

entry.title: Prefer:odata.continue-on-error - Not working in ODATA V4 BATCH API call
entry.content:
Hi,

While Postman correctly returns both success and error responses for all payloads when the Prefer: odata.continue-on-error header is used, this same header fails to function as expected within the Fiori app's AJAX call. 

Case 1 : Suppose if all 5 payloads are successful, API gives a response for all 5 payloads .

Case 2: Total 3 payloads,  1st payload response is successful, 2  payload response fails with 400 error and skips 3rd payload response.




CAse 2 analysis - The API continues to return the first successful response and the second failed response, but then skips the third payload entirely.

FIORI APP SOURCE CODE

var URL = "/sap/opu/odata4/sap/-------------------/";

var msgarr = [];

$.ajax({

    url: URL,

    type: "POST",

    contentType: "multipart/mixed;boundary=batch",

    headers: {

        "X-CSRF-Token": UCSRFToken,

        "Prefer": "odata.continue-on-error"

    },

    data: sPayload,

    success: function (data, textStatus, jqXHR) {

        var a = data;

        var sResponse = jqXHR.responseText;

    }

});

Issue Summary: Why is the Prefer header not working in SAP Web IDE? Despite adding the header to the code above, the application still terminates processing after a payload failure, skipping subsequent responses.

Thanks,

Madhava

entry.title: Issue in Direct GRN automatic PO Net price
entry.content:
Hello Consultants, 

Recently, we have encountered an issue regarding direct GRN's automatic Purchase order fetches the previous PO's net price instead of the updated price for the random materials (not all materials). 

So, it's a sequential updating process. First, we update the info record using BAPI 'ME_INFORECORD_MAINTAIN_MULTI', and we include it in the wrapper function module (RFC). Second, we are posting GRN (101) using BAPI 'BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE'.  

The whole process runs in the OData api and it has been integrated with an external third-party application.  We post the goods movement (using above mentioned BAPIs) in SAP once we receive the payload data from the external system. 

These two BAPIs are executed sequentially per post call triggered from the external system. Hence, we used a wrapper function module for info record update, and cross check the info record and condition price related database tables (EINE, A067, and KONP) after execution of the info record update BAPI.  Now, we have two scenarios as follows,

i) Sometimes the database tables didn't update with the correct price for random materials, even though we used transaction commit BAPI, and wait upto 10 secs logic.

ii) Sometimes the database is updated with the correct price. However, the GRN BAPI auto PO picks the previous day's net price for random materials. 

Note: There are no custom enhancements at the backend, and the configurations have been checked along with MM consultants.

I am looking forward to your responses.

Many thanks in advance!

entry.title: RAP Draft: Side Effects for Associated Text Fields not reflecting in UI (Fiori Elements OData V4)
entry.content:
What I'm trying to achieve ? 

When the user types or selects a new VehicleNumber on the Create/Edit screen:

  1. A backend determination finds the corresponding DriverID.

  2. The UI automatically refreshes the DriverID, DriverName, and DriverMobile fields on the screen.

What I have implemented ?

1. Behvaior definition : Defined the side effects to trigger a refresh of all three fields when the vehicle field changes.

// Side effects
  side effects {
    field VehicleNumber
      affects field DriverID, 
              field DriverName, 
              field DriverMobile;
  }

  determination FillDriverDetails on modify { field VehicleNumber; }

2. Determination Logic 'FillDriverDetails' : Initially, I only updated the DriverID, expecting the SADL engine to perform the join and fetch the name/mobile during the subsequent read.

" Reading master data for the vehicle to take the assigned the DriverID
  
  MODIFY ENTITIES OF ZI_TripHeader IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY TripHeader
    UPDATE FIELDS ( DriverID )
    WITH VALUE #( ( %tky     = <ls_trips>-%tky
                    DriverID = ls_driver-d_id ) ).

3. The Netwrok behaviour: When changing the Vehicle Number in UI, the side effects work perfectly on the frontend.
        a. UI sends a PATCH with the new VehicleNumber.
        b. UI immediately sends a GET request explicitly asking for DriverID, DriverName and DriverMobile ($select=DriverMobile,DriverName)

The Problem : The DriverID is getting updated with the new value in the UI. But, the Driver Name and Mobile number fields are getting the old value (value previously available or empty) and the UI is not reflecting the change. 

To my perspective, the side effect is working as expected. But the framework is not resolving the associated field values of Driver CDS view.

  • UI: SAP Fiori Elements (OData V4)

  • Entities: ZI_TripHeader (Root) with associations to ZI_Vehicle and ZI_Driver and a composition relationship for ZI_TripIncidents.

  • DriverName and DriverMobile are read-only fields in the root view, pulled via association (_Driver.FullName, _Driver.Mobile).

Below is the CDS view entities.
1. ZI_TripHeader (root Entity)

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Root entity for Trip transaction'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
define root view entity ZI_TripHeader
  as select from ztab_tripheader
  composition [0..*] of ZI_TripIncident as _Incident
  association [1] to zi_driver          as _Driver  on $projection.DriverID = _Driver.DriverId
  association [1] to ZI_Vehicle         as _Vehicle on $projection.VehicleNumber = _Vehicle.VehicleRegNumber
  association [1] to ZI_LoadTypeVH as _LoadTypeVH on $projection.LoadType = _LoadTypeVH.LoadType
{
  key t_tripid              as TripID,
      t_cdate               as TripDate,
      t_vno                 as VehicleNumber,
      t_driverid            as DriverID,
      t_source              as StartPlace,
      t_destination         as EndPlace,
      
      t_loadtype            as LoadType,
      t_status              as TripStatus,
      t_tot_exp             as TripTotalExpense,
      
      @Semantics.user.createdBy: true
      created_by            as CreatedBy,
      
      @Semantics.systemDateTime.createdAt: true
      created_at            as CreateAt,
      
      @Semantics.user.lastChangedBy: true
      last_changed_by       as LastChangedBy,
      
      @Semantics.systemDateTime.lastChangedAt: true
      last_changed_at       as LastChangedAt,
      
      @Semantics.systemDateTime.localInstanceLastChangedAt: true
      local_last_changed_at as LocalLastChangedAt,

      // ---> below two associated fields are not resolved for UI Refresh
      _Driver.FullName      as DriverName,
      _Driver.Mobile        as DriverMobile,
      
      _LoadTypeVH.Description as LoadDescription,

      // Make association public
      _Incident,
      _Driver,
      _Vehicle,
      _LoadTypeVH
}

2. ZI_Driver (associated entity)

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Interface view for driver'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Search.searchable: true
define root view entity zi_driver
  as select from ztab_driver
{
      @EndUserText.label: 'ID'
  key d_id        as DriverId,
  
      @EndUserText.label: 'Name'
      @search.defaultSearchElement: true
      d_full_name as FullName,    // this one
      
      @EndUserText.label: 'Phone'
      d_mobile    as Mobile,      // this one
      
      @EndUserText.label: 'Emergency'
      d_emobile   as Emergencymobile,
      
      @EndUserText.label: 'License'
      d_license   as DrivingLicense,
      
      @EndUserText.label: 'Expiry'
      d_lexpiry   as LicenseExpiry,
      
      @EndUserText.label: 'Govt.ID'
      d_govid     as GovernmentID,
      
      @EndUserText.label: 'Aadhar'
      d_aadhar    as AadharCard,
      
      @EndUserText.label: 'PAN Card'
      d_pan       as PanCard,
      
      @EndUserText.label: 'Status'
      d_status    as DriverStatus
}

The Root and child entities are draft enabled.

Am I missing anything important detail for the framework to resolve the values of associated fields ? I'm expecting experts inputs and opinions. Thanks in advance.

Screenshot of DraftTable - Added upon Junwu comment

Best Regards,
M Boopalan

entry.title: Anyone using AI agents with SAP OData in production — what’s breaking the most right now?
entry.content:
Genuine question for anyone running SAP + AI agents in production or POC:

I've been trying to figure out the actual technical failure modes of LLM agents on SAP OData — separate from the loud Policy v4/2026 debate.

In my own testing on the ES5 sandbox, even simple business questions like "find all open sales orders for a customer" produced wrong OData URLs more often than I expected. Hallucinated field names (SalesOrderID instead of SalesOrder), wrong navigation paths, unsupported $filter syntax.

A few things I'd genuinely value input on:

1. If you've built something that works reliably against real S/4HANA (not sandbox) — what made the difference? Knowledge
graphs, fine-tuning, semantic layer, something else?

2. What's the failure mode you keep hitting that nobody's written about? The quiet ones.

3. For European customers under DSAG's flag — how are you actually thinking about Policy v4/2026 architecturally? Are you waiting, pivoting, or building something compliant?

 Trying to map the real terrain before committing more weekends to it.

entry.title: Fiori Elements ALP (OData V2): Basic Search field shown despite @Search.searchable: false in CDS
entry.content:
Problem:

In an Analytical List Page (ALP) application based on a directly exposed CDS view, the Basic Search field is displayed in the SmartFilterBar, even though search is explicitly disabled by using the CDS Annotation: @search.searchable: false.

Analytical List Page (OData V2, UI5 1.71, SAP_BASIS 750)

Expectation: Basic Search should be hidden.
Actual behavior: Basic Search is still displayed.

Important:

  • No @search.* annotations on field level
  • Only @search.searchable: false on view level

Findings
The behavior appears to contradict the expected semantics of the annotation.
In this setup (SAP_BASIS 750 + UI5 1.71 + OData V2), the system behaves as follows:

The mere presence of the annotation @search.searchable (regardless of its value) seems to activate search capabilities in the OData service and UI.

As a result:

The SmartFilterBar interprets the service as search-enabled
The Basic Search field is displayed, even when set to false

Workaround
Remove (or comment out) the annotation completely
@search.searchable: false

Side Effect

After removing the annotation, the CDS view produces a syntax/activation warning or error similar to:
"@Search annotations require 'searchable' for the view"

However:

If the CDS is still activated (ignoring the warning)
The resulting behavior is that the Basic Search field is no longer displayed

entry.title: ABAP Trial: OData V4 service binding shows Published but runtime says service group not published
entry.content:
I am experiencing the same issue today in SAP BTP ABAP Trial, system TRL, client 100, while working through SAP Learning “Basic ABAP Programming”.
I also commented on a similar existing thread, but I am opening a separate question because I have a reproducible case with a working old binding and a failing newly created binding on the same RAP BO.
An existing OData V4 UI service binding still works: ZUI_98FLIGHT_O4
To isolate the issue, I created a new OData V4 UI service binding on the same working RAP BO / service definition: ZUI_96FLIGHT_O4
ADT shows: Local Service Endpoint: Published
The generated inbound service also exists: ZUI_96FLIGHT_O4_0001_G4BA
However, the Test fails with:
"Service group 'ZUI_96FLIGHT_O4' not published"
The ADT Feed Reader / Gateway Error Log shows:
  • Package: /IWFND/V4_COS_PUBLISHING
  • Application Component: BC-ESI-ESF-GW
  • Request Kind: Server OData V4
  • Service Name: ZUI_96FLIGHT_O4
  • Service Version: 0001
  • Group ID: ZUI_96FLIGHT_O4
Request URI: /sap/opu/odata4/sap/zui_96flight_o4/srvd/sap/zui_96flight_o4/0001/$metadata?sap-documentation=all
Exception: /IWBEP/CX_V4_ACCESS_CHECK
  • Text: Service group 'ZUI_96FLIGHT_O4' not published
  • HTTP_STATUS_CODE: 404
  • Raise Location: /IWFND/CL_V4_PUBLISHING_DBA
The source extract shows that the runtime checks /IWFND/C_V4_MSGR:
SELECT SINGLE group_id FROM /iwfnd/c_v4_msgr
      INTO lv_group_id
      WHERE group_id = iv_group_id.
 
This check fails although ADT shows the service binding as Published.
Conclusion:
It looks like the ADT publish action updates the service binding object, but the OData V4 runtime publication entry is not created in /IWFND/C_V4_MSGR.
Old service bindings continue to work, but newly created service bindings do not.
Has anyone found a workaround for the ABAP Trial system?
One additional detail: a few days ago I ran “Check for Updates” in Eclipse and updated my ADT/Eclipse installation. I am not sure whether this is related, but the issue started after that timeframe.

entry.title: Error calling the OData V4 adapter for a RAP Odata service
entry.content:
When invoking the OData V4 adapter using the PATCH operation from the Integration Suite, I receive the following error: "EDMX Fetch failed with HTTP Response Code: 403." The OData service is based on a custom-developed managed RAP object.
The READ operation works, and directly invoking the OData service with PATCH via Postman also works. Therefore, I suspect this is an issue specific to the Integration Suite. What could be the cause?

entry.title: BAdI for Filter in Retail Fiori app Order Products
entry.content:
The Retail Fiori App Order Products (F0752) can be extended with custom filters using BAdI's BADI_RTST_OP_FILTER and BADI_RTST_OP_FILTERVALUE. This works fine; filters and their values appear in the app. When selecting a filter value however, the app breaks with message "Property x not found in type StoreProduct" because the filter field is not available in the metadata of the service. The app is based on SEGW service RETAILSTORE_ORDER_PRODUCT.  

The documentation off these BAdI's gives the following information:
For each new filter, a new corresponding property has to be created in the entity StoreProduct of the OData Service RETAILSTORE_ORDER_PRODUCT_SRV. The name of the property has to correspond to the ID of the filter so that the UI can map the filter value to the respective property. A new field also has to be created in the structure RTST_OP_S_STORE_PRODUCT_INCL that must be mapped to the new property in the entity.

There seems to be no BAdI in the SEGW service itself to add the property so how can the property be added in a clean way? Creating a custom service to redefine the standard service appears to be the only way to manage custom filters. Or am I overlooking an option?

entry.title: altSuperID is null
entry.content:
In SuccessFactors Learning ODATA API I am trying to obtain alternate supervisor IDs for a given user.

Sadly, despite the supervisors being set in the LMS, the altSuperID1, 2 and 3 fields are null instead of holding the userIDs.

SAP Support replied with a KB Article which says it's expected, because a user could have even up to 200 alternate managers and that the only way out is an enhancement request.

I wanted to ask if anyone has hit this obstacle in alt super IDs and worked around it?

--- Steps to Reproduce ---

  1. Go to LMS, search for a user, add an alternate manager to that user.
  2. Obtain LMS Token for Admin
  3. Call /learning/odatav4/user/v1/Users('userID')
  4. Observe all altSuperID are null. At least one of them should be populated as it is in the LMS.

entry.title: OData vs SOAP for .NET WMS Integration in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I am seeking technical advice regarding the integration of a .NET-based WMS solution developed by our team with  SAP Business ByDesign.

As we move away from historical on-premise setups toward a decoupled 'Side-by-Side' architecture, we want to use the app with SAP Public Cloud . I have two critical questions for those who have managed similar cloud transitions:

1. API Utilization for .NET Integration
Since our WMS middleware is built on .NET, we need to ensure robust communication with the SAP core.

  • Which specific OData services or SOAP APIs are recommended for real-time warehouse tasks (e.g., Goods Receipt, Stock Transfer, Picking)in SAP Public Cloud ?
  • For those using SAP Public Cloud, are you primarily using the SiteLogisticsTask or WarehouseConfirmation web services, and how do they perform under the high-frequency request load typical of a .NET scanning application?

2. Public Cloud WM Capability & Responsiveness
We are evaluating how the Warehouse Management (WM) module in the Public Cloud responds to external 'exploits' or calls from a third-party .NET interface.

  • Does the Public Cloud WM module offer the same level of flexibility as the historical on-premise WM/EWM when it comes to API-triggered movements?
  • Are there known performance limitations or 'throttling' issues when a .NET application performs high-volume scanning directly against the Public Cloud tenant?
  • In your experience, does the Public Cloud WM architecture provide sufficient coverage for complex scenarios when managed via external APIs?

We want to ensure that our project respects the 'Clean Core' strategy while maintaining the historical reliability that  our company has built into the  App over the years.

I would greatly appreciate any technical documentation, API maps, or feedback from your own cloud WMS implementations.

Best regards,

entry.title: How to add custom fields to Fiori App Manage Customer Line Items?
entry.content:
Dear Community,

i want to add custom fields to the Fiori App Manage Customer Line Items F0711.

We are on private cloud 2025 initial shipment.

I noticed that there is an oData v2 Service: FAR_CUSTOMER_LINE_ITEMS and that i should use a Fiori Adaption Project.

So my idea is to extend the original oData Service and add my custom fields. Afterwards create a Adaption Project for the App and change the oData Service to the modified version.

But i dont know how to add custom Fields to this oData Service. I know that i can add fields in SEGW but i dont know how to fill them with data.

Any help is greatly appreciated.

Kind regards

Marcel

entry.title: WMS API Equivalents in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition vs. SAP Business ByDesign
entry.content:
We are currently working with a customer who uses a .NET-based solution integrated with SAP Business ByDesign.

In the current ByD setup, warehouse operators work based on logistics tasks generated by the system (e.g., Goods Receipt, Stock Transfer, Picking). Our WMS consumes these tasks and allows operators to execute and confirm them. This process is supported through APIs such as SiteLogisticsTask and WarehouseConfirmation.

The customer is now evaluating a transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and would like to maintain the same operational model.

 

While we understand that S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition provides Warehouse Management capabilities and supports API-based integration with external systems, we are struggling to identify the equivalent of the ByD logistics task concept.

 

Our questions are:

  • Is there an equivalent to ByD Logistics Tasks / Warehouse Tasks in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
  • Can these tasks be created, retrieved, processed, and confirmed through standard APIs by an external WMS?
  • Which business objects and APIs should be considered as the equivalent of SiteLogisticsTask and WarehouseConfirmation in ByD?
  • Are there any limitations or recommended approaches when implementing a task-driven warehouse execution process with an external WMS in Public Cloud?

Our objective is to offer the customer the same warehouse execution experience they currently have in ByD while following SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition best practices and Clean Core principles.

Any guidance, architecture recommendations, or references to relevant APIs and business objects would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Assistance Required: OData API Errors on SAP CAL FPS01 2025
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community, I am currently testing API integrations on an SAP CAL FPS01 2025 system and have encountered blockers with three standard OData services. I have done some initial troubleshooting but need guidance on standard resolutions or missing configurations.

Financial Planning API

When attempting a POST request to API_FINPLANNINGDATA_SRV, I receive a message stating the CREATE_ENTITY method is not implemented in the data provider class. I suspect this requires ABAP intervention in the DPC_EXT class under CO-FIO-OM-PL. Is there a standard SAP Note to regenerate the runtime objects for this service, or is manual ABAP implementation strictly required to enable POST operations?

Fixed Asset API

I cannot locate the Fixed Asset APIs in the OData V2 catalog, though I see API_FIXEDASSETACQUISITION_G4BA exists in OData V4. Have all Fixed Asset APIs strictly migrated to V4 in the 2025 release? If not, is there a recommended V2 alternative that does not require building Custom CDS Views as an External API?

Enterprise Project API

Posting to version 2 of API_ENTERPRISE_PROJECT_SRV results in an ABAP short dump OBJECTS_OBJREF_NOT_ASSIGNED_NO due to a dereferenced null object. I believe this is caused by stricter mandatory field requirements in version 2 compared to version 1. Could someone provide the absolute minimal working JSON/XML payload required for v2, or point me to a patch note for this specific dump?

API Status Matrix

 
Service Name Error Encountered Suspected Resolution Path
API_FINPLANNINGDATA_SRVCREATE_ENTITY not implementedSEGW regeneration or ABAP developer overriding DPC_EXT
Fixed Asset APINot found in OData V2 catalogSwitch client integration to OData V4 endpoints
API_ENTERPRISE_PROJECT_SRVOBJECTS_OBJREF_NOT_ASSIGNED_NOCorrecting missing payload fields or applying SAP Note

entry.title: Gatewayb builder ODATA for odp throws error
entry.content:
We get an error while trying to publish and ODP Source via Gateway Builder as ODATA

Process to repeat

  • SEGW
  • create project
  • right click on Datamodel of new project
  • select "Redifine" - "ODP Extraction"
  • enter RFC Destination: DB4CLNT500, ODP Context: BW BW/4HANA, ODP Name: VP0FSACT1$F
  • click add ODP
  • click Next
  • enter empty field Service Regsitration - Description: "TEST"
  • Error appears: Make an entry in mandatory field "Description"  Message No. 00278

    Process can not be finished

Can anybody help?

entry.title: Fiori Elements V2 (Adaptation Project) – Need to enforce mandatory field validation before Save/Appl
entry.content:
Hello SAP community,

I am working on a Fiori Elements V2 application (Object Page / List Report) using an Adaptation Project (SAP BAS – codeExt Controller Extensions + UI Adaptation).

I am trying to enforce a business requirement where a specific field must be mandatory before saving the document.

 

Technical Context

  • Application: Standard SAP Fiori Elements V2 app (Object Page)
  • Extension approach: Adaptation Project (no full custom controller, only Controller Extensions via codeExt)
  • Backend service: OData V2 service
  • Field source: Edm.Decimal field in OData entity
  • Field name: NoDaysReminder1
  • Annotation applied:
 
com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1.FieldControl = Mandatory
  • Field also appears inside a FieldGroup in Object Page sections.

 Observed Behavior

  • The field is displayed as mandatory in the UI (red asterisk appears correctly).
  • However, the application still allows saving the document when the field is empty.
  • No frontend validation blocks the Save/Apply action.

 What I already tried

1. Controller Extension (codeExt)

I attempted to override lifecycle hooks in the Object Page extension:

  • beforeSaveExtension
  • onAfterRendering
  • Custom validation logic

However, none of these hooks are triggered during Save/Apply in this Adaptation Project context.

2. UI interception attempts

I also tried:

  • Intercepting the Apply button (OverflowToolbar / DOM inspection)
  • Using event delegates on SmartField/Input controls
  • Using extensionAPI and TransactionController

None of these approaches allowed reliable interception of the Save event.

3. Metadata analysis

The OData metadata shows:

 

 
<Property Name="NoDaysReminder1"
          Type="Edm.Decimal"
          sap:field-control="NoDaysReminder1_fc" />

The field is NOT marked as Nullable="false", so from a service contract perspective it is optional.

Key Issue

It seems that:

  • FieldControl annotation only affects UI behavior
  • OData V2 service contract allows null values
  • Fiori Elements V2 does not expose a supported Save-interception hook in Adaptation Projects

Question to the community

Question to the community

Given the limitations of Fiori Elements V2 Adaptation Projects, what is the recommended SAP-supported pattern to enforce mandatory field validation BEFORE Save/Apply for a standard Object Page?

Specifically:

1. Is there any supported Fiori Elements V2 extension point (within Adaptation Projects / codeExt) that allows intercepting or validating the Save/Apply action?

2. If frontend-only enforcement is not supported, what is the recommended SAP backend approach for enforcing mandatory business validation in OData V2 services (e.g., BAdI, enhancement spots, gateway validations)?

3. In standard SAP design, should FieldControl be considered only a UI indication, or can it ever enforce transactional validation without backend support?

Additional context

This requirement is strictly frontend-driven at the moment, as backend modifications are currently uncertain or restricted.

Additional technical detail:

The application is part of SAP standard S/4HANA Fiori Elements V2 (Object Page) and is extended via SAP Business Application Studio Adaptation Project.

The extension is limited to controller extension artifacts generated via @SAP-ux/adp tooling (codeExt), meaning:

- No full override of standard controller lifecycle
- No access to Fiori Elements internal Save lifecycle hooks
- Limited access to extensionAPI hooks only

I am trying to confirm whether this is a limitation of the Adaptation Project framework or a misunderstanding of the available extension points in Fiori Elements V2.

Any clarification from SAP experts or official pattern references would be highly appreciated.

entry.title: Alternative to ChangeDocumentCollection for Service Request checklist change history in SSC
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

we need to extract checklist change history for Service Requests from SAP Service Cloud into SAP Datasphere.
Currently we use: /sap/c4c/odata/v1/changedoclist/ChangeDocumentCollection

with the mandatory filter: BusinessObject eq 'ServiceRequest'
and a date filter such as: ChangeDateTime ge datetime'2025-11-01T00:00:00'

The service provides the information we need, especially old/new values and change metadata:
ChangeDateTime
ObjectNodeElementName
ObjectNodeElementOldContent
ObjectNodeElementNewContent

However, the extraction is very slow and runs into timeouts in SAP Datasphere. 

Extracting the data with date filter such as: ChangeDateTime ge datetime'2026-06-01T00:00:00' is running successful, but we need the data starting from 2025-11-01T00:00:00'.

Is there a better OData service/entity for Service Request checklist change history?
Or is ChangeDocumentCollection the only standard source for old/new value changes?

Is there a supported way to apply a monthly date range filter on ChangeDateTime in the ChangeDocumentCollection?

Thanks!

Best regards
Julian

SAP Datasphere OData SAP Service Cloud
